# PERF-161 positive: rows.Next() loop without rows.Err() check
lang: go
file: PERF-161-vulnerable.go
variant: stdlib
---
package sample

import "database/sql"

type User struct {
	ID   int
	Name string
}

func LoadUsers(db *sql.DB) ([]User, error) {
	rows, err := db.Query("SELECT id, name FROM users")
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	defer rows.Close()
	var users []User
	for rows.Next() {
		var u User
		if err := rows.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Name); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		users = append(users, u)
	}
	return users, nil
}
